atlas.put_in: spatial prisoner's dilemma (Nowak-May): payoff C-C=1, D-from-C=b; copy the best neighbor
emerged: cooperator fraction: well-mixed=0.0 (collapses), spatial=0.731 (survives in clusters)
assortment=0.126 (>0: cooperators cluster); b-sweep (spatial)=[(1.4, 0.72), (1.7, 0.001), (1.85, 0.0), (2.0, 0.0), (2.3, 0.0)] (collapses beyond b~1.7)
surprises: spatial structure alone lets cooperation resist cheaters; well-mixed always collapses
atlas.persistence: spatial cooperation holds while the temptation b is bounded; beyond ~1.7 it collapses
atlas.not_scripted_check: cooperator fraction and assortment are measured; only the payoff + update rules are set
atlas.claim_tier: measured (cooperator fraction, assortment) ; interpretive (a higher-level unit forms when individual & group interest align via assortment) ; analogy (major transition / multicellularity)
floors: cooperation STABILITY (necessary condition), NOT a full major transition
group-level reproduction, division of labor, conflict suppression (bottlenecks/policing) are frontier
the temptation b sits in a survival band (collapses beyond ~1.7); 'major transition' is analogy
